text:
Admission Date:  2013-01-20              Discharge Date:   2000-02-28

Date of Birth:  2000-11-27             Sex:   F

Service: NEUROSURGERY

Allergies:
Patient recorded as having No Known Allergies to Drugs

Attending:Nicholas
Chief Complaint:
SAH, Unresponsive


Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
Left Pcom aneursym coiling
Right frontal ventricular catheter placement
Left subclavian TLC placement
Right axillar A-line

History of Present Illness:
57yo F s/p syncopal episode at work this morning at approx
0800. Tx. to OSH, CT demonstrated massive SAH. Medflighted to
Healthsouth Rehabilitation Hospital Of The Mid-cities for further treatment.


Past Medical History:
Migraines, Smoker


Social History:
Married
Smoker

Family History:
NC

Physical Exam:
O: BP: 133/71 on Dopamine, Levo  HR: 95  R:20T  O2Sats:100%
Gen: Intubated, Unresponsive
HEENT: normocephalic, atraumatic. Pupils: 3-2.5 Bilat. Does not
open eyes.  no corneal reflex.
Extrem: Warm and well-perfused.
Neuro:
Mental status: Intubated, unresponsive

Cranial Nerves:
I: Not tested
II: Pupils equally round and reactive to light, 3 to 2.5
mm bilaterally. No corneal reflex.

Motor: Normal bulk and tone bilaterally. Trace withdrawal only
all extremities.


Pertinent Results:
2013-01-20 10:50AM   ASA-NEG ETHANOL-NEG ACETMNPHN-NEG
bnzodzpn-NEG barbitrt-NEG tricyclic-NEG
2013-01-20 10:50AM   CK-MB-10 MB INDX-5.0

2013-01-20 12:31PM   LACTATE-1.6
2013-01-20 05:45PM   CK-MB-16* MB INDX-7.4* cTropnT-0.86*
2013-01-20 05:45PM   CK(CPK)-217*
2013-01-20 09:06PM   LACTATE-3.8*
CT/CTA: 1995-04-29
1. Large subarachnoid hemorrhage as described above.
2. 10 x 9 mm aneurysm arising from left supraclinoid carotid
artery and 3 x 3
mm aneurysm arising from right supraclinoid carotid artery.A
third 2-3 mm
aneurysm is seen at the left intracranial ICA bifurcation.
3. Developing hydrocephalus.
ANGIO 2013-01-20:
successful near complete coiling of ruptured 8 x 9mm left PCOM
origin
aneursym. Small 2.5 x 2.3 cm right posterior choroidal aneursym
noted with no intervention performed.
CXR: 2007-11-21:
 In comparison with the study of 05-23, there is continued
prominence
of ill-defined pulmonary vessels consistent with elevated
pulmonary venous
pressure. Poor definition of the left hemidiaphragm suggests
some pleural
fluid on this side. No definite focal pneumonia, though there
could be a
suggestion of consolidation at the left base that should be
followed on
subsequent images.

CTA/P HEAD W&W/O C & RECONS 1992-12-11
1. No evidence of acute infarction or vasospasm.
2. Unchanged diffuse subarachnoid hemorrhage without significant
mass effect or shift of normally midline structures or
hydrocephalus. Cerebral edema.

CTA/P HEAD W&W/O C & RECONS 1993-12-03
Mild vasospam, left > right MCA which more pronounced than the
previous CTA of 1992-12-11. No perfusion abnormalities.

BILAT LOWER EXT VEINS 1973-07-29
No DVT.

CTA/P HEAD W&W/O C & RECONS 1996-07-08
1. Findings most consistent with mild vasospasm within the M1
and M2
segments, slightly increased on the right and unchanged on the
left.
2. Stable subarachnoid hemorrhage and right posterior
parafalcine
interhemispheric subdural hemorrhage. Intraventricular
hemorrhage not well
visualized on this examination. No evidence of hydrocephalus.
3. No abnormal differential perfusion identified.



Brief Hospital Course:
This patient was admitted from the ED non responsive with a
aneurysmal SAH.  Pt. was taken directly to the angio suite where
she was found to have Pcom aneursym was coiled. She was admitted
to the ICU for twelve days. Significant events during her ICU
stay included + troponins requiring vasopressor support. An echo
showed depressed mild LVEF circumfrential thought to related to
pressors. She received an external ventriculostomy drain for
which she had periods of high ICPs that were treated with
mannitol and sedation. She eventually developed fevers the
presumptive source of the fever was felt to be ventriculitis due
CSF results WBC 1bb34c31-40a6-3f11-dbf9-2f1bc55046c4 RBC8450 Polys 77 Bands 1 Lymphs1 Monos 21
Pro 31 Gluc 96 Gram neg 4+PMNL's there was no growth from the
CSF. She is receiving a 14 day course of Vancomycin and
Meropenum for treatment. Her EVD was removed on 03-27 with no
further fever spikes.

The patients mental status while in ICU was semicomatose where
she would follow in consistent commands and required prolonged
ventilation for which she got a Trach on 12-06.

Imaging of her brain showed questionable vasospasm via CTA and
follow-up angiogram on 05-13 showed a stable coiling of the P-Comm
aneurysm.

The patient was transferred to our neuro-step down on 01-04 she
passed her speach and swallow on 03-27 the diet recommendation
was puree with nectar thick liquids and ree only when patient
100% attentive and participating in POs. When stimulated Mrs
Fisher will awaken when sitting up will take full meals.

On discharge she was awake, alert, orientated X1 hypophonic will
follow simple commands and is antigravity in all extremities.


Medications on Admission:
Unknown

Discharge Disposition:
Extended Care

Facility:
Midwest Surgical Hospital Llc Medical Center - Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center - West Campus

Left PCOM aneurysm
+ MI

Discharge Condition:
Neurologically stable


Discharge Instructions:
Medications:
??????	Take Aspirin 325mg (enteric coated) once daily.
??????	Continue all other medications you were taking before surgery,
unless otherwise directed
??????	You make take Tylenol or prescribed pain medications for any
post procedure pain or discomfort.

What activities you can and cannot do:
??????	When you go home, you may walk and go up and down stairs.
??????	You may shower (let the soapy water run over groin incision,
rinse and pat dry)
??????	Your incision may be left uncovered, unless you have small
amounts of drainage from the wound, then place a dry dressing or
band aid over the area that is draining, as needed
??????	No heavy lifting, pushing or pulling (greater than 5 lbs) for
1 week (to allow groin puncture to heal).
??????	After 1 week, you may resume sexual activity.
??????	After 1 week, gradually increase your activities and distance
walked as you can tolerate.
??????	No driving until you are no longer taking pain medications

What to report to office:
??????	Changes in vision (loss of vision, blurring, double vision,
half vision)
??????	Slurring of speech or difficulty finding correct words to use
??????	Severe headache or worsening headache not controlled by pain
medication
??????	A sudden change in the ability to move or use your arm or leg
or the ability to feel your arm or leg
??????	Trouble swallowing, breathing, or talking
??????	Numbness, coldness or pain in lower extremities
??????	Temperature greater than 101.5F for 24 hours
??????	New or increased drainage from incision or white, yellow or
green drainage from incisions
??????	Bleeding from groin puncture site

*SUDDEN, SEVERE BLEEDING OR SWELLING
(Groin puncture site)
Lie down, keep leg straight and have someone apply firm pressure
to area for 10 minutes. If bleeding stops, call our office. If
bleeding does not stop, call 911 for transfer to closest
Emergency Room!


Followup Instructions:
Follow up in 4 weeks with Dr Craig with a CTA; please call
293-365-8101 to have this appointment scheduled
